mechanism_narrative: INDPRO rises over trailing 3 months indicate expanding industrial output, which raises corporate earnings forecasts and drives S&P 500 positive returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns is non-positive over any 5-year rolling window, or a strategy long when INDPRO is above median fails to beat buy-and-hold over 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE > 0.4 percentage points over 3 months) signals economic deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.4 percentage points over a rolling 3-month period
failure_condition: When triggered, the average 20-day forward SPX return is non-negative or the proportion of negative returns is less than 50%, measured over all historical triggers.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low (FEDFUNDS < 2%) and CPI inflation is accelerating (CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.5%), it indicates that monetary policy is too loose relative to real economy, creating inflation risk that will force future tightening and depress equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS < 2% and CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.5%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is not negative on average (t-test p <= 0.05), or the signal generates less than 5% frequency over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS below 2%), a strong pickup in inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year surpassing 3%) warns that the central bank will soon tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 2%)
failure_condition: When both regime and trigger conditions hold, the average 20-day SPX return is non-negative or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% over all instances.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month indicates strong consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and drives S&P 500 positive returns over the next 5 trading days as markets quickly adjust.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS monthly percent change and next 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over any 5-year window, or the long-short portfolio based on RSAFS deciles has negative Sharpe ratio over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth over the last two quarters reflects the fundamental strength of the economy; above-trend GDP growth boosts long-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC between GDPC1 year-over-year growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window, or the information ratio of a strategy long when GDP growth is above median is negative over the out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.5 million units monthly, it signals robust construction and housing market strength, which spills over into broad economic activity and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1500 thousand units in a month
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the proportion of positive returns is less than 60% over all triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the trailing 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, which raises the discount rate for equities and reduces present values of future cash flows, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is non-negative over any 5-year window, or if the average return following FEDFUNDS increases is not negative.
